questions.q: $\int_{-\alpha}^a e^x+\cos x \ln \left(x+\sqrt{1+x^2}\right) d x\gt\frac{3}{2}$, then $\alpha$ can be
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{I}=\int_{-\alpha}^\alpha \mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x}} \mathrm{dx}+\int_{-\alpha}^\alpha \cos \mathrm{x} \ln \left(\mathrm{x}+\sqrt{1+\mathrm{x}^2}\right) \mathrm{dx}$
$\Rightarrow \mathrm{e}^\alpha-\mathrm{e}^{-\alpha}\gt\frac{3}{2}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $u_n=\int_0^{\pi / 2} \cos ^n x \cos n x d x$ it turns out that $u_1, u_2, u_3, \ldots$. form a G.P. then
options: the common ratio of G.P. is $\frac{1}{2}$
the common ratio of G.P. is $\frac{1}{4}$
questions.solution:
First use integrating by parts to show that
$\begin{aligned}
& u_n=\int_0^{\pi / 2} \cos ^{n-1} x \sin x \sin n x d x \\
& =\int_0^{\pi / 2} \cos ^{n-1} x \cos (n-1) x-\int_0^{\pi / 2} \cos ^n x \cos n x d x(\text { Use } \sin n x \sin x=\cos (n-1) x-\cos n x \cos x) \\
& =u_{n-1}-u_n \\
& \Rightarrow \frac{u_n}{u_{n-1}}=\frac{1}{2}
\end{aligned}$
We have, $u_1=\frac{\pi}{4}, u_n=\frac{1}{2^{n-1}} \cdot \frac{\pi}{4}=\frac{\pi}{2^{n+1}}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The integral $\int_{\tan -1}^{\cos }-\frac{\tan x}{\tan x+\cot x} d x, \forall \lambda \in R$ can not take the value.
questions.solution:
$I=\int_{\tan ^{-1} \lambda}^{\cos ^{-1} \lambda} \frac{\tan x}{\tan x+\cot x} d x$    ....(i)
$=\int_{\tan ^{-1} \lambda}^{\cot \lambda} \frac{\cot x}{\tan x+\cot x} d x$     .....(2)
$2 \mathrm{I}=\cot ^{-1} \lambda-\tan ^{-1} \lambda \Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\frac{\pi}{4}-\tan ^{-1} \lambda$
Now, $\frac{-\pi}{2} \lt \tan ^{-1} \lambda \lt \frac{\pi}{2}$
$-\frac{\pi}{2} \lt -\tan ^{-1} \lambda \lt \frac{\pi}{2} \Rightarrow-\frac{\pi}{4} \lt 1 \lt \frac{3 \pi}{4}$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $f(x)=2+\int_{-1}^1\left(\frac{t x^2}{2}+\frac{9 x}{14}\right) f(t) d t$, then:
options: Rolle's Theorem is applicable for $y=f(x)$ in $[-2,-1]$
maximum value of $f(x)$ does not exist
questions.solution:
$f(x)=2+\frac{x^2}{2} \int_{-1}^1 t f(t) d t+\frac{9 x}{14} \int_{-1}^1 f(t) d t$
Let $A=\frac{1}{2} \int_{-1}^1 t f(t) d t$ and $B=\frac{9}{14} \int_{-1}^1 f(t) d t$
$\begin{aligned} & \therefore \quad f(x)=A x^2+B x+C \\ & \therefore \quad A=\frac{1}{2} \int_{-1}^1 t\left(A t^2+B t+2\right) d t=\int_0^1 B t^2 d t=\frac{B}{3} \Rightarrow 3 A=B\end{aligned}$
and $B=\frac{9}{14} \int_{-1}^1\left(A t^2+B t+2\right) d t=\frac{9}{14} \times 2\left(\frac{A t^3}{3}+2 t\right)_0^1$
$\begin{aligned}& \Rightarrow \quad 3 A=\frac{9}{7}\left(\frac{A}{3}+2\right)=\frac{3}{7}(A+6) \\& \Rightarrow \quad 21 A=3 A+18 \Rightarrow A=1 \text { and } B=3 \\& \therefore \quad f(x)=x^2+3 x+2
\end{aligned}$
Now, verify options.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Given a function $f: R \rightarrow R$ defined as $f(x)=\left[\begin{array}{ll}x, & x < 0 \\ \sin x, & 0 \leq x \leq \pi / 2 \\ 1, & x>\pi / 2\end{array}\right.$ 
If $f(x)=a \int_0^{\pi / 2}|x-t| \sin t d t+b x+c$, then:
questions.solution:
$f(x)=\left[\begin{array}{lc}x, & x < 0 \\ \sin x, & 0 \leq x \leq \pi / 2 \\ 1, & x>\pi / 2\end{array}=a \int_0^{\pi / 2}|x-t| \sin t d t+b x+c\right.$
If $x < 0, \quad x=a \int_0^{\pi / 2} t \sin t d t-a x \int_0^{\pi / 2} \sin t d t+b x+c \Rightarrow a+c=0 \quad$ and $\quad b-a=1$
If $x>\frac{\pi}{2}, \quad 1=a x \int_0^{\pi / 2} \sin t d t-a \int_0^{\pi / 2} t \sin t d t+b x+c \Rightarrow c-a=1$ and $a+b=0$
$\Rightarrow \quad a=\frac{-1}{2} ; \quad b=c=\frac{1}{2}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $Y=\int_0^1 \frac{2 x^2+3 x+3}{(x+1)\left(x^2+2 x+2\right)} d x$. Then which of the following option(s) are equal to $Y$ ?
questions.solution:
$Y=\int_0^1 \frac{2 x^2+3 x+3}{(x+1)\left(x^2+2 x+2\right)} \quad$ By partial fraction decomposition
$\begin{aligned} & =\int_0^1\left(\frac{2}{x+1}-\frac{1}{x^2+2 x+2}\right) d x=\int_0^1\left(\frac{2}{x+1}-\frac{1}{(x+1)^2+1}\right) d x \\ & =2 \ln (x+1)-\left.\arctan (x+1)\right|_0 ^1 \\ & =2 \ln 2-\arctan 2+\frac{\pi}{4} \Rightarrow\end{aligned}$
From (a)
$A=2 \ln 2+\frac{\pi}{4}-\arctan 2=2 \ln 2+\arctan \left(\frac{1-2}{1+2}\right)=2 \ln 2-\operatorname{arccot} 3 \Rightarrow(c)$
From (a)
$A=2 \ln 2+\frac{\pi}{4}-\arctan 2=-\frac{\pi}{4}+\ln 4+\frac{\pi}{2}-\arctan 2=-\frac{\pi}{4}+2 \ln 2+\operatorname{arccot} 2 \Rightarrow(d)$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $f(x)=\int_1^x \frac{\ln t}{1+t} d t$, then
questions.solution:
$f\left(\frac{1}{x}\right)=\int_1^{1 / x} \frac{\ln t}{(1+t)} d t=\int_1^x \frac{-\ln u}{\frac{1+u}{u}} \times \frac{-1}{u^2} d u=\int_1^x \frac{\ln t}{t(1+t)} d t$
Now, $f(x)+f\left(\frac{1}{x}\right)=\int_1^x \frac{\ln t}{(1+t)}\left(1+\frac{1}{t}\right) d t=\int_1^x \frac{\ln t}{t} d t=\left[\frac{(\ln t)^2}{2}\right]_1^x=\frac{(\ln x)^2}{2}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Which of the following definite integral vanishes?
options: $\int_{-\pi}^\pi\left(\cos 2 x \cdot \cos 2^2 x \cdot \cos 2^3 x \cdot \cos 2^4 x \cdot \cos 2^5 x\right) d x$
questions.solution:
(1) $I=\int_{-\pi}^\pi \underbrace{\left(\cos 2 x \cdot \cos 2^2 x \cdot \cos 2^3 x \cdot \cos 2^4 x \cdot \cos 2^5 x\right)}_{f(x)} d x$
$I=2 \int_0^\pi f(x) d x \quad[f(x)$ is given $]$
$I=2 \cdot 2 \int_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}} f(x) d x$ (using Queen)
$I=4 \int_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}} f(x) d x=4 I_1$ 
Now, $\quad I_1=\int_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}} f(x) d x$
Using King
$\begin{aligned} & I_1=-I_1 \\ & \therefore \quad I_1=0 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad I=0\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $I_n=\int_{-1}^1|x|\left(1+x+\frac{x^2}{2}+\frac{x^3}{3}+\ldots \ldots+\frac{x^{2 n}}{2 n}\right) d x$, then:
questions.solution:
$I_n=2 \int_0^1 x\left(1+\frac{x^2}{2}+\frac{x^4}{4}+\ldots . .+\frac{x^{2 n}}{2 n}\right) d x \quad\left(\because \int_{-1}^1(\right.$ odd $\left.) d x=0\right)$
$\begin{aligned} & =2\left(\frac{x^2}{2}+\frac{x^4}{2 \cdot 4}+\frac{x^6}{4 \cdot 6}+\ldots . .+\frac{x^{2 n+2}}{2 n(2 n+2)}\right)_0^1 \\ & =1+\frac{1}{2}\left[\left(1-\frac{1}{2}\right)+\left(\frac{1}{2}-\frac{1}{3}\right)+\ldots \ldots+\left(\frac{1}{n}-\frac{1}{n+1}\right)\right] \\ & =1+\frac{1}{2}\left(1-\frac{1}{n+1}\right)\end{aligned}$
$I_2=1+\frac{1}{2}\left(1-\frac{1}{3}\right)=\frac{4}{3} \quad$ and $\quad I_{\infty}=\frac{3}{2}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $L=\frac{\int_0^{n \pi} e^{-x}\left(\sin ^4 a x+\cos ^2 a x\right) d x}{\int_0^\pi e^{-x}\left(\sin ^4 a x+\cos ^2 a x\right) d x}$, where $a \in R$ then:
questions.solution:
Let $f(x)=e^{-x}\left(\sin ^4 a x+\cos ^2 x\right)$
$\therefore \quad \int_0^{n \pi} f(x) d x=\int_0^\pi f(x) d x+\underbrace{\int_\pi^{2 \pi} f(x) d x}_{x=\pi+t}+\underbrace{\int_{2 \pi}^{3 \pi} f(x) d x}_{x=2 \pi+t}+\ldots \ldots+\underbrace{\int_{(n-1) \pi}^{n \pi} f(x) d x}_{x=(n-1) \pi+t}$
$\begin{array}{ll} & =\int_0^\pi f(x) d x\left(1+e^{-\pi}+e^{-2 \pi}+\ldots \ldots .+e^{-(n-1) \pi}\right) \text { for } a \in I \\ \therefore \quad & L=1+e^{-\pi}+e^{-2 \pi}+\ldots \ldots .+e^{-(n-1) \pi} \forall a \in I \\ \therefore \quad & \lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} L=\frac{1}{1-e^{-\pi}}>1 \forall a \in I\end{array}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
If $f(x) \int \frac{\tan ^3 x}{2+\tan ^2 x} d x=\ln \left|\frac{2-g(x)}{\cos x}\right|+C$, where $f(0)=\ln 2$ and $C$ is the constant of integration, then:
[Note: $[k]$ denotes greatest integer function less than or equal to $k$.]
questions.solution:
$\int \frac{\left(\sec ^2 x-1\right) \tan x}{\tan ^2 x+2} d x=\int\left(\frac{\sec ^2 x \tan x}{\sec ^2 x+1}-\frac{\tan x}{\sec ^2 x+1}\right) d x$
Put $\sec x=t \Rightarrow \sec x \tan x d x=d t$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \int\left(\frac{t}{t^2+1}-\frac{1}{t\left(t^2+1\right)}\right) d t=\int\left(\frac{t}{t^2+1}-\left(\frac{1}{t}-\frac{t}{t^2+1}\right)\right) d t=\int\left(\frac{2 t}{t^2+1}-\frac{1}{t}\right) d t \\
& =\ln \left(t^2+1\right)-\ln t+C=\ln \left(\frac{\sec ^2 x+1}{\sec x}\right)+C=\ln \left(\frac{1+\cos ^2 x}{\cos x}\right)+C=\ln \left(\frac{2-\sin ^2 x}{\cos x}\right)+C \\
& g(x)=\sin ^2 x
\end{aligned}
$$
Now, verify the options.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.q: Let $I_1=\int_0^1 \sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{x}{\sqrt{1+x^2}}\right) d x ; I_2=\int_0^1 \cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{x}{\sqrt{1+x^2}}\right) d x$ and $I_3=\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty}\left(\frac{1}{n+1}+\frac{1}{n+2}+\ldots \ldots+\frac{1}{2 n}\right)$, then:
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & I_1=\frac{\pi}{4}-\frac{1}{2} \ln 2 \\ & I_2=\frac{\pi}{4}+\frac{1}{2} \ln 2 \\ & I_3=\ln 2\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $f(x)=\int_{-1}^1(1-|t|) \cos (x t) d t$ then which of the following hold true?
options: $\operatorname{Lim}_{x \rightarrow 0} f(x)$ exists and is equal to 1
questions.solution:
$f(x)=2 \int_0^1 \underbrace{(1-t)}_I \underbrace{\cos (x t)}_{I I} d t=2\left[\left.(1-t) \frac{\sin x t}{x}\right|_0 ^1+\frac{1}{x} \int_0^1 \sin x t d t\right]=2\left[0-\left.\frac{1}{x^2} \cos x t\right|_0 ^1\right]$
$f(x)=2\left[\frac{1-\cos x}{x^2}\right](x \neq 0)$
if $x=0$ then $f(x)=\int_{-1}^1(1-|t|) d t=2 \int_0^1(1-t) d t=1$
$\Rightarrow$ (C) is correct
hence $f(x)=\left[\begin{array}{c}2\left(\frac{1-\cos x}{x^2}\right) \text { if } x \neq 0 \\ 1 \\ 1\end{array}\right.$
$\therefore \quad \mathrm{f}$ is continuous at $\mathrm{x}=0$
$\Rightarrow$ (D) is correct
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The value of $\sum_{k=1}^{\infty} \frac{6^k}{\left(3^k-2^k\right)\left(3^{k+1}-2^{k+1}\right)}$ can be equal to:
questions.solution:
Let $3^k=x$
$2^k=y$
$\therefore \quad \frac{6^k}{\left(3^k-2^k\right)\left(3^{k+1}-2^{k+1}\right)}=\frac{x y}{(x-y)(3 x-2 y)}$
$\begin{aligned} & =\frac{y(3 x-2 y)-2 y(x-y)}{(x-y)(3 x-2 y)}=\frac{y}{x-y}-\frac{2 y}{3 x-2 y} \\ & =\frac{2^k}{3^k-2^k}-\frac{2^{k+1}}{3^{k+1}-2^{k+1}}\end{aligned}$
$\therefore \quad \sum_{k=1}^{\infty} \frac{6^k}{\left(3^k-2^k\right)\left(3^{k+1}-2^{k+1}\right)}=\sum_{k=1}^{\infty} \frac{2^k}{3^k-2^k}-\frac{2^{k+1}}{3^{k+1}-2^{k+1}}$
$\begin{aligned} & T_1=\frac{2}{3-2}-\frac{2^2}{3^2-2^2} \\ & T_2=\frac{2^2}{3^2-2^2}-\frac{2^3}{3^3-2^3} \\ & \vdots \\ & T_k=\frac{2^k}{3^k-2^k}-\frac{2^{k+1}}{3^{k+1}-2^{k+1}}\end{aligned}$
$\sum_{k=1}^{\infty} T_k=\lim _{k \rightarrow \infty}\left(2-\frac{2 \cdot 2^k}{3 \cdot 3^k-2 \cdot 2^k}\right)=2$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let a function $f: R \rightarrow R$ be defined as $f(x)=x+\sin x$ and $I=\int_0^\pi f^{-1}(x) d x$ then:
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \because \int_0^\pi f(x) d x+\int_0^\pi f^{-1}(x) d x=\pi^2 \\ & \text { and } \int_0^\pi f(x) d x=\int_0^\pi(x+\sin x) d x=\left(\frac{x^2}{2}-\cos x\right)_0^\pi=\frac{\pi^2}{2}+2 \\ & \therefore\left(\frac{\pi^2}{2}+2\right)+\int_0^\pi f^{-1}(x) d x=\pi^2\end{aligned}$
$\begin{array}{ll}\therefore & I=\frac{\pi^2}{2}-2 \\ \therefore & 2 < I < 3 \\ \because & \int_0^1 \frac{1}{1+x^3} d x < \int_0^1 1 d x < 1 < I\end{array}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $f: R \rightarrow(0, \infty)$ be a real valued function satisfying $\int_0^x t f(x-t) d t=e^{2 x}-2 x-1$, then which of the following is(are) correct?
options: Derivative of $f(x)$ with respect to $e^x$ at $x=0$ is equal to 8
The value of $f(0)$ is equal to 4
questions.solution:
$\int_0^x t f(x-t) d t=e^{2 x}-1$
Using King
$\begin{aligned} & \int_0^x(x-t) f(t) d t=e^{2 x}-1 \\ & x \int_0^x f(t) d t-\int_0^x t f(t) d t=e^{2 x}-1\end{aligned}$
Differentiate both sides
$x f(x)+\int_0^x f(t) d t-x f(x)=2 e^{2 x}-2 \Rightarrow f(x)=4 e^{2 x} \Rightarrow f(0)=4$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $f(x)+g(x)+h(x)=2 \forall x \in R$, then the value of the expression $\int_0^{3 / 4}\left(f^2(x)+g^2(x)+h^2(x)\right) d x$, can be:
questions.solution:
Let $\overrightarrow{\mathbf{v}}_1=\hat{\mathbf{i}}+\hat{\mathbf{j}}+\hat{\mathbf{k}} \quad$ and $\quad \overrightarrow{\mathbf{v}}_2=f(x) \hat{\mathbf{i}}+g(x) \hat{\mathbf{j}}+h(x) \hat{\mathbf{k}}$
Now, $\quad \overrightarrow{\mathbf{v}_1} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathbf{v}}_2=2=\left|\overrightarrow{\mathbf{v}}_1\right|\left|\overrightarrow{\mathbf{v}}_2\right| \cos \theta=\sqrt{3} \sqrt{f^2(x)+g^2(x)+h^2(x)} \cos \theta$
Hence, $\quad \frac{4}{3} \sec ^2 \theta=f^2(x)+g^2(x)+h^2(x) \geq \frac{4}{3}$
Hence, $\quad I_{\min }=\int_0^{3 / 4}\left(f^2(x)+g^2(x)+h^2(x)\right) d x=\int_0^{3 / 4} \frac{4}{3} d x=1$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $f(x)$ and $g(x)$ be two derivable function on $R$ (the set of all real numbers) satisfying $f(x)=\frac{x^3}{2}+1-x \int_0^x g(t) d t$ and $g(x)=x-\int_0^1 f^{\prime}(t) d t$, then:
options: number of points of non-derivability of $f(|x|)$ is zero
number of points of non-derivability of $f(|x|)$ is one
questions.solution:
Let $k=\int_0^1 f(t) d t$
$\begin{array}{ll}\therefore & g(x)=x-k \\ \therefore & f(x)=\frac{x^3}{2}+1-x \int_0^x(t-k) d t=\frac{x^3}{2}+1-x\left(\frac{x^2}{2}-k x\right)=1+k x^2 \\ \therefore & k=\int_0^1 f(t) d t=\left(t+\frac{k t^3}{3}\right)_0^1 \Rightarrow k=1+\frac{k}{3} \Rightarrow k=\frac{3}{2} \\ \therefore & f(x)=1+\frac{3 x^2}{2}\end{array}$
$\therefore \quad f(|x|)=1+\frac{3 x^2}{2}$ which is always derivable.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $X=\sum_{x=0}^n\left(\frac{(-1)^r}{r+1}\right)$, then $|X-\ln 2|$ will be
questions.solution:
$1-x+x^2-x^3+\ldots+(-x)^n=\frac{1+(-1)^n x^{n+1}}{1+x}$
$\Rightarrow \int_0^1\left(1-x+x^2-x^3+\ldots+(-x)^n\right) d x=\int_0^1\left(\frac{1}{1+x}\right) d x+(-1)^n \int_0^1\left(\frac{x^{n+1}}{1+x}\right) d x$
$\Rightarrow|X-\ln 2|=\int_0^1\left(\frac{x^{n+1}}{1+x}\right) d x$, where $\frac{1}{2(n+2)} \lt \int_0^1\left(\frac{x^{n+1}}{1+x}\right) d x \lt \frac{1}{2(n+1)}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $f(x)=\int_0^x[f(x)]^{-1} d x$ and $\int_1^1\left[\left.f(x)\right|^{-1} d x=\sqrt{2}\right.$, then
questions.solution:
$f(x)=\int_a^x \frac{1}{f(x)} d x \Rightarrow f(x)=\frac{1}{f(x)} \cdot 1-0 \Rightarrow f(x) f(x)=1$
$\Rightarrow \int \mathrm{f}(\mathrm{x}) \mathrm{f}^{\prime}(\mathrm{x}) \mathrm{dx}=\int 1 \mathrm{dx} \Rightarrow \frac{(\mathrm{f}(\mathrm{x}))^2}{2}=x+c$   ....(1)
Given $\int_a^1[f(x)]^{-1} d x=\sqrt{2} \Rightarrow f(1)=\sqrt{2}$
From (1), $\frac{(f(1))^2}{2}=1+c \Rightarrow c=0$
$f(x)= \pm \sqrt{2 x}$
But $f(1)=\sqrt{2} \Rightarrow f(x)=\sqrt{2 x} \Rightarrow f(2)=2$
$f^{\prime}(x)=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2 x}} \Rightarrow f^{\prime}(2)=\frac{1}{2}$
$\int_0^1 f(x) d x=\frac{2^{3 / 2}}{3}$
$f^{-1}(x)=\frac{x^2}{2} \Rightarrow f^{-1}(2)=2$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
